paper.title: Horizontal Delaunay surfaces with constant mean curvature in $\mathbb{S}^2\times\mathbb{R}$ and $\mathbb{H}^2\times\mathbb{R}$

global_context.setup: Fix κ ∈ ℝ and a horizontal geodesic Γ ⊂ 𝕄²(κ) × {0}. Consider constant mean curvature H > 0 such that 4H² + κ > 0. The goal is to construct a family of complete H-surfaces in 𝕄²(κ) × ℝ that are invariant under discrete translations along Γ and lie at bounded distance from Γ.
global_context.target_theorem: Fix κ∈ℝ and a horizontal geodesic Γ⊂𝕄²(κ)×{0}. There exists a family Σ_{λ,H}^*, parametrized by λ≥0 and H>0 such that 4H²+κ>0, of complete H-surfaces in 𝕄²(κ)×ℝ, invariant under a discrete group of translations along Γ with respect to which they are cylindrically bounded. They are symmetric about 𝕄²(κ)×{0} and Γ×ℝ. Moreover: (i) Σ_{0,H}^* is the invariant H-cylinder (H-torus if κ>0); (ii) for 0<λ<π/2, Σ_{λ,H}^* is the unduloid from [MT]; (iii) Σ_{π/2,H}^* is a stack of tangent rotationally invariant H-spheres; (iv) for λ>π/2, Σ_{λ,H}^* is a nodoid-type surface.

paper.title: Sixfolds of generalized Kummer type and K3 surfaces

global_context.setup: Let $K$ be a hyper-Kähler sixfold of generalized Kummer type ($\mathrm{Kum}^3$-type). Let $\Aut_0(K)$ be the group of automorphisms of $K$ acting trivially on $H^2(K,\mathbb{Z})$. Let $G\subset \Aut_0(K)$ be the subgroup generated by automorphisms whose fixed locus contains a 4-dimensional component; $G\cong (\mathbb{Z}/2\mathbb{Z})^5$. Consider the quotient $K/G$, which is singular.
global_context.target_theorem: Let $K$ be a manifold of $\mathrm{Kum}^3$-type. The quotient $K/G$ admits a resolution $Y_K\to K/G$ with $Y_K$ a manifold of $\mathrm{K}3^{[3]}$-type.

local_context: We sketch the proof of Theorem \ref{thm:MainResult}. First, we calculate the fixed loci $K^g$ of all the automorphims $g\in G$: we show that $\bigcup_{g\neq 1 \in G} K^g$ is the union of $16$ hyper-K\"{a}hler manifolds of $\mathrm{K}3^{[2]}$-type, and we determine the various intersections of these components. Thanks to the deformation invariance of $G$, it is in fact enough to calculate these loci in the special case of the generalized Kummer sixfold on an abelian surface.

We then describe explicitly the singularities of $K/G$. It turns out that these are of a particular simple nature, modeled on products of ordinary double points on surfaces. A resolution $Y_K$ of $K/G$ is obtained via a single blow-up of the singular locus. The quotient $K/G$ is a primitive symplectic orbifold as studied by Fujiki~\cite{fujiki1983} and Menet~\cite{menet}, and we use a criterion due to Fujiki to prove that $Y_K$ is a hyper-K\"ahler manifold. Moreover, the resolution can be performed in families, so that the~$Y_K$ are deformation equivalent to each other. To complete the proof of Theorem~\ref{thm:MainResult} it is therefore sufficient to find a single~$K$ of $\mathrm{Kum}^3$-type such that $Y_K$ is of $\mathrm{K}3^{[3]}$-type.

The specific example we study is a Beauville-Mukai system $K_J(v_3)$ on a general principally polarized abelian surface $\Theta\subset J$. It admits a Lagrangian fibration to the linear system $|2\Theta|=\PP^3$, whose general fibres parametrize certain degree $3$ line bundles supported on curves in the linear system. We show that the norm map for line bundles induces a dominant rational map of degree $2^5$ from $K_J(v_3)$ onto a Beauville-Mukai system $M_{\Km(J)}(w_3)$, a moduli space of sheaves on the Kummer $\mathrm{K}3$ surface $\Km(J)$ associated to~$J$; the hyper-K\"{a}hler variety $M_{\Km(J)}(w_3)$ is birational to ${\Km(J)}^{[3]}$. paper.title: Stochastic homogenization for variational solutions of Hamilton-Jacobi equations

global_context.setup: Probability space (Ω,μ) with an ergodic action τ of ℝⁿ. Hamiltonian H(x,p;ω) on T*ℝⁿ is stationary: H(x+a,p;τ_a ω)=H(x,p;ω). Study stochastic Hamilton-Jacobi equation (HJS_ε) with initial condition f. Goal: homogenization as ε→0 to an effective Hamiltonian \overline{H}.
global_context.target_theorem: (Main theorem). Let H(x,p;ω) satisfy conditions (C1)-(C6): (C1) τ is measure preserving and ergodic; (C2) stationarity; (C3) H is C^{1,1} in (x,p) a.s.; (C4) uniform fiber compact support; (C5) bound on ∂H/∂p; (C6) bound on H. Then the flow φ^t_{ε,ω} of H_ε(x,p)=H(x/ε,p) converges in γ_c topology to \overline{φ}^t, the flow of \overline{H} in the completion. Consequently, for uniformly continuous f, the variational solution u^ε(t,x;ω) converges to the variational solution \overline{u}(t,x) of the homogenized equation.

paper.title: A transference principle for systems of linear equations, and applications to almost twin primes

global_context.setup: We study admissible affine-linear maps Ψ: ℤ^d → ℤ^t of finite complexity (no two forms affinely related) and subsets P of primes, specifically almost twin primes (Chen primes or bounded gap primes) and primes of the form x^2+y^2+1. The problem is to find n ∈ ℤ^d such that Ψ(n) ∈ P^t, i.e., solve linear systems in these sparse sets. A transference principle is developed to transfer results from dense sets to such sparse pseudorandom sets.
global_context.target_theorem: For almost twin primes and primes of the form x^2+y^2+1, the existence of solutions to finite complexity systems of linear equations is determined by natural local conditions.

paper.title: A Cheeger–Müller theorem for manifolds with wedge singularities

global_context.setup: Let \bar N be a compact oriented manifold with boundary, endowed with a fiber bundle structure φ: ∂\bar N → Y with fiber Z, where Y is a compact oriented manifold. Let N = interior(\bar N). The corresponding stratified space \widehat{N} is obtained by collapsing the fibers of φ. Let r ∈ C^∞(\bar N) be a boundary defining function. A product-type wedge metric g_w on N is defined in a tubular neighborhood as g_w = dr^2 + r^2 κ + φ^* g_Y, where κ is a symmetric 2-tensor on ∂\bar N restricting to a Riemannian metric on each fiber, and g_Y is a Riemannian metric on Y. Consider a flat vector bundle F over N and the Hodge Laplacian Δ_q acting on forms with values in F. Assume the Hodge Laplacians on the fibers have an appropriate spectral gap (no non-zero eigenvalues sufficiently close to zero).
global_context.target_theorem: \begin{theorem} Let $\eth_{\ew}$ be the wedge surgery de Rham operator associated to a choice of exact $\ew$-metric and a choice of flat vector bundle $F\to M$ with bundle metric. Suppose that $\eth_{\ew}$ satisfy Assumption~\ref{ur.1}. Then for any bounded open set $V\subset \bbC$ such that $$ \overline{V}\cap \spec(N_{\mf}(\eth_{\ew}))\subset \{0\}, $$ there exists $\tau>0$ and a holomorphic family $V\ni \lambda\mapsto f(\cdot,\lambda) \in\CI([0,1]_{\epsilon})+\cA^\tau([0,1])$ such that the resolvent $(D_{\ew}^2-\lambda)^{-1}$ extends from $V\cap(\bbC\setminus\bbR)$ to a meromorphic family $$ (D_{\ew}^2-\lambda)^{-1}= \Res_H(\lambda)+ \Res_M(\lambda) $$ on $V$, with only simple poles, where $\lambda\to \rho^{-1}\Res_H(\lambda)\rho^{-1}\in \Psi^{-2,\tau}_{\ee}(X_s;E)$ is a holomorphic family, while $\Res_M(\lambda)$ is a meromorphic family uniformly of finite rank such that $$ \lambda\to f(\epsilon,\lambda)\rho^{-1}\Res_M(\lambda)\rho^{-1}\in \Psi^{-\infty,\tau}_{\ee}(X_s;E) $$ is a holomorphic family uniformly and such that $$ N_{\mf}(\Res_M(\lambda))= -\frac{\Pi_{\ker_{L^2} N_{\mf}(D^2_{\ew})}}{\lambda}, \quad N_{\ff}(\rho^{-1}\Res_M(\lambda)\rho^{-1})=0. $$ \label{ur.8}\end{theorem}

paper.title: Generalized cusps in real projective manifolds: classification

global_context.setup: A generalized cusp is a properly convex projective manifold C = Ω/Γ, where Ω ⊂ ℝℙ^n is a properly convex set, Γ ⊂ PGL(n+1,ℝ) is a virtually abelian discrete group preserving Ω, ∂C is compact and strictly convex, and there is a diffeomorphism h: [0,∞) × ∂C → C. For ψ: ℝ^n → ℝ with ψ(e1) ≥ ψ(e2) ≥ ⋯ ≥ ψ(en) ≥ 0, there is a properly convex domain Ω(ψ) ⊂ ℝℙ^n. For ψ ≠ 0, the cusp Lie group G(ψ) = PGL(Ω(ψ)), and for ψ = 0, it is the subgroup of non-hyperbolic elements. A ψ-cusp is Ω(ψ)/Γ for a lattice Γ in G(ψ). Two generalized cusps are equivalent if there exists a generalized cusp C'' and projective embeddings that are homotopy equivalences of C'' into both. G(ψ) = T(ψ) ⋊ O(ψ), where T(ψ) ≅ ℝ^{n-1} is the translation subgroup and O(ψ) ⊂ O(n-1) fixes a point in ∂Ω. Define cmlat(G) as the set of G-conjugacy classes of marked lattices in G, and let cmlat(Isom(𝔼^{n-1}), ψ) ⊂ cmlat(Isom(𝔼^{n-1})) be the subset of conjugacy classes of marked Euclidean lattices with rotational part of the holonomy (up to conjugacy) in O(ψ).
global_context.target_theorem: \begin{theorem}[Classification]\label{Classification Theorem} \ \ \begin{enumerate} \item[(i)] If Γ and Γ' are lattices in G(ψ) TFAE \begin{enumerate} \item Ω(ψ)/Γ and Ω(ψ)/Γ' are equivalent generalized cusps \item Γ and Γ' are conjugate in PGL(n+1,ℝ) \item Γ and Γ' are conjugate in PGL(Ω(ψ)) \end{enumerate} \item[(ii)] A lattice in G(ψ) is conjugate in PGL(n+1,ℝ) into G(ψ') iff G(ψ) is conjugate to G(ψ'). \item[(iii)] G(ψ) is conjugate in PGL(n+1,ℝ) to G(ψ') iff ψ' = t·ψ for some t > 0. \item[(iv)] PGL(Ω(ψ)) = G(ψ) when ψ ≠ 0 \item[(v)] When ψ ≠ 0 the map θ: cmlat(Isom(𝔼^{n-1}), ψ) × (O(n-1)/O(ψ)) → cmlat(G(ψ)) defined in (\ref{thetadef}) is a bijection. \end{enumerate} \end{theorem}
